title: Remove ContactPatchOp enum: direct fields beat intermediate vec for 2 variants
description: The ContactPatchOp enum had exactly 3 variants mapping to 2 fields (blocked: Option<bool>, display_name: Option<Option<String>>). Collecting into a Vec<ContactPatchOp> then decomposing it back to local variables in Phase 2b added indirection with zero payoff — the decomposition loop was just reconstruction. Direct field assignment is simpler, removes an allocation, and the atomicity guarantee (RFC 8620 §7.1) is unchanged because Phase 1 still runs entirely before Phase 2. A future contributor might suggest restoring the enum 'for extensibility' — resist this unless there are 4+ variants or the ops need to carry richer metadata.
close_reason: Decision records — no code change needed; recorded for future reference
